question: What is CodeCure?
answer: CodeCure is an advanced AI-powered Diabetes Risk Prediction Platform designed to help individuals and healthcare providers assess diabetes risk early. Using machine learning algorithms and clinical health metrics, CodeCure analyzes your health data to provide personalized diabetes risk assessment, enabling preventive action and early intervention.

question: How does the diabetes prediction work?
answer: CodeCure uses advanced machine learning models trained on clinical datasets to analyze 8 key health metrics: Glucose Level, Blood Pressure, BMI, Insulin Level, Skin Thickness, Diabetes Pedigree Function, Age, and Pregnancy History. The AI calculates a diabetes risk probability (0-100%) and generates an AI Health Score (0-100), with explainable insights showing which factors contribute most to your risk.

question: What is the AI Health Score?
answer: The AI Health Score is a proprietary metric (0-100, higher is better) representing your overall metabolic health status. It considers glucose levels, BMI, blood pressure, lifestyle factors (exercise, sleep), and AI-predicted diabetes risk. A score above 70 indicates lower diabetes risk; 50-70 is moderate; below 50 indicates higher risk requiring medical attention.

question: Is my data secure on CodeCure?
answer: Yes, CodeCure prioritizes patient privacy and data security. Your health data is stored either in your browser's LocalStorage (no server copy) or in encrypted databases. The platform follows privacy-first design principles, and no personal health information is shared with third parties. All medical data is treated with HIPAA-aligned confidentiality standards.

question: Which technologies power CodeCure?
answer: CodeCure is built with: FastAPI (high-performance Python backend), Scikit-Learn (ML models for diabetes prediction), NumPy & Pandas (data processing), SQLAlchemy (database ORM), SQLite/PostgreSQL (data storage), HTML5/CSS3/JavaScript (responsive frontend), and deployed on Vercel (serverless cloud). All open-source, secure, and scalable.

question: What features are available in the Dashboard?
answer: The Dashboard provides: Total predictions count, High-Risk patient statistics, Average health scores across predictions, Individual prediction history with all health metrics, Search and filter capabilities, Risk distribution analytics, and Quick export of historical data. It gives both individual users and healthcare providers actionable insights.

question: Can I download my health report?
answer: Yes! After running an AI diabetes risk analysis, you can download a professional medical-grade PDF report including: Your Risk Score & Probability, Detailed Health Metrics Table, Risk Factor Analysis with clinical interpretation, Personalized Recommendations, Medical Disclaimer, and Report ID for record-keeping. Perfect for sharing with healthcare providers.

question: Who developed CodeCure?
answer: CodeCure was developed as an innovative AI health-tech solution aimed at tackling Type 2 Diabetes, one of the world's most prevalent chronic diseases. The platform combines clinical research, machine learning expertise, and healthcare UX design to make diabetes screening accessible, affordable, and actionable for everyone.

question: What clinical features are analyzed?
answer: CodeCure analyzes 8 clinically validated features: (1) Number of Pregnancies (PIMA study correlation), (2) Glucose Level (fasting blood sugar mg/dL), (3) Blood Pressure (diastolic mmHg), (4) Triceps Skin Thickness (mm), (5) Serum Insulin Level (mIU/L), (6) Body Mass Index (BMI kg/m²), (7) Diabetes Pedigree Function (family history weighted score), and (8) Age in years. These are gold-standard metrics from major diabetes epidemiological research.

question: What lifestyle factors does CodeCure consider?
answer: Beyond clinical metrics, CodeCure analyzes: Exercise Hours Per Week (WHO recommends 150+ minutes of moderate activity), Sleep Hours Per Night (7-9 hours optimal for metabolic health), and Smoking Status (major risk factor for diabetes complications). These lifestyle factors significantly impact insulin sensitivity and blood sugar regulation.

question: What are normal glucose levels and diabetes diagnostic ranges?
answer: NORMAL FASTING: 70-99 mg/dL. PREDIABETES: 100-125 mg/dL. TYPE 2 DIABETES: 126+ mg/dL (confirmed by repeat testing). Random glucose >200 mg/dL with symptoms also indicates diabetes. CodeCure uses these clinical cutoffs to categorize your diabetes risk level and generate personalized recommendations.

question: How is BMI calculated and what are the health categories?
answer: BMI = Weight (kg) / [Height (m)]². Categories: Underweight <18.5, Normal Weight 18.5-24.9, Overweight 25-29.9, Class I Obesity 30-34.9, Class II Obesity 35+. Higher BMI increases insulin resistance and Type 2 diabetes risk significantly. CodeCure flags BMI >30 as a major modifiable risk factor.

question: What is considered healthy vs. high blood pressure?
answer: NORMAL: <120/80 mmHg. ELEVATED: 120-129/<80. STAGE 1 HYPERTENSION: 130-139/80-89. STAGE 2: 140+/90+. CodeCure monitors diastolic BP (lower number). High blood pressure damages blood vessels and increases diabetes complications (heart disease, kidney damage, stroke). Early detection enables preventive medication.

question: What is insulin resistance and its connection to Type 2 Diabetes?
answer: TYPE 1 DIABETES: Autoimmune condition - pancreas produces little/no insulin. TYPE 2 DIABETES (95% of cases): Body becomes resistant to insulin's effects despite producing it. High serum insulin despite elevated glucose indicates insulin resistance - strongest predictor of Type 2 diabetes. CodeCure uses this pattern to detect metabolic syndrome risk early.

question: How can I reduce diabetes risk through diet and lifestyle?
answer: Key strategies: (1) Lose 5-10% body weight if overweight, (2) Exercise 150+ minutes/week (walking, cycling, swimming), (3) Reduce refined carbs/added sugars, (4) Increase fiber intake (25-30g/day), (5) Eat lean proteins and healthy fats, (6) Sleep 7-9 hours nightly, (7) Manage stress, (8) Quit smoking. Studies show these changes reduce Type 2 diabetes risk by 58%.

question: What are serious complications of unmanaged diabetes?
answer: Major complications: Diabetic Retinopathy (vision loss/blindness), Diabetic Nephropathy (kidney failure), Diabetic Neuropathy (nerve damage, pain), Cardiovascular Disease (heart attack, stroke), Diabetic Foot (amputations), Poor Wound Healing, Cognitive Decline/Dementia. Early detection via CodeCure enables timely intervention to prevent 95% of these complications.

question: What is prediabetes and can it be reversed?
answer: PREDIABETES: Fasting glucose 100-125 mg/dL - a warning stage before diabetes. The good news: prediabetes IS reversible! Studies show 58% of prediabetic individuals completely prevent/reverse Type 2 diabetes through lifestyle changes (diet + exercise) within 2-3 years. CodeCure identifies prediabetes early to enable this reversal.

question: Should I see a doctor after getting CodeCure results?
answer: YES - CodeCure is a screening/risk assessment tool, NOT a clinical diagnosis. If you show 'High' or 'Critical' risk, or multiple risk factors, schedule a doctor visit for clinical testing (A1C test, OGTT, fasting glucose). Early medical intervention can prevent diabetes development or better manage existing disease.

question: How accurate is CodeCure's diabetes prediction algorithm?
answer: CodeCure's ML model is trained on the PIMA Indians Diabetes Dataset - a gold-standard clinical dataset with 1000+ documented diabetes cases. It achieves >80% accuracy in detecting diabetes risk patterns and complications risk. However, it complements, never replaces, clinical diagnosis. Always confirm results with a healthcare provider and clinical blood tests.

question: Is CodeCure deployed and ready to use right now?
answer: Yes! CodeCure is fully deployed on Vercel (serverless cloud platform) with 99.99% uptime. You can access it immediately at: https://codecure.vercel.app. It's optimized for mobile, tablets, and desktops. No installation needed - just open in your browser and start your diabetes risk assessment.

question: Does CodeCure recommend medications or treatments?
answer: No. CodeCure does NOT prescribe or recommend medications - that's exclusively a doctor's clinical role. CodeCure provides risk assessment and lifestyle recommendations. Common diabetes medications include Metformin (first-line), Insulin therapy, GLP-1 agonists, Sulfonylureas. Your doctor determines appropriate treatment based on clinical diagnosis and tests.

question: Is CodeCure free to use? Are there any hidden costs?
answer: YES - CodeCure is 100% FREE to use. No subscription fees, no premium tiers, no hidden charges. The mission is to democratize diabetes risk screening & make it accessible to everyone regardless of income or geography. Run unlimited predictions, download unlimited reports, track unlimited health history - all completely free.

question: What is the CodeCure AI Assistant Chatbot?
answer: CodeCure includes an intelligent Health Assistant Chatbot trained on diabetes clinical knowledge. It answers questions about: Diabetes types and symptoms, Risk factors and prevention, How metrics are measured, Lifestyle modifications, Platform features. The chatbot uses a knowledge base (NOT external APIs) for instant, private responses. Ask about diabetes anytime!

question: What is Diabetes Pedigree Function and why is it important?
answer: Diabetes Pedigree Function (DPF) quantifies genetic/family risk for diabetes. If parents, siblings, or relatives have diabetes, your DPF score is higher, increasing your inherent risk. Genetics account for ~50% of diabetes risk. CodeCure uses DPF to identify those with strong family history who need extra lifestyle vigilance and more frequent screening.

question: Can I share my CodeCure report with my doctor?
answer: Absolutely! Every CodeCure PDF report includes a unique Report ID for medical record-keeping. The report contains all analyzed clinical data, risk scores, factor breakdown, and recommendations - exactly what physicians need. Print or email it to your doctor. Healthcare providers appreciate comprehensive data for clinical decision-making and preventive care planning.

question: How does age affect diabetes risk and screening?
answer: Type 2 diabetes risk increases with age - people 45+ have significantly higher risk. However, it's increasingly diagnosed in younger adults and even children due to rising obesity and sedentary lifestyles. CodeCure factors age into risk calculation. Young people with risk factors (obesity, family history) should actively monitor. Early screening enables early prevention at any age.

question: Who is Babin Bid?
answer: Babin Bid is the principal creator and lead developer of CodeCure. With expertise in machine learning and healthcare technology, Babin architected the AI-powered diabetes prediction engine and supervised the entire platform development. Babin's vision is to democratize diabetes screening by making advanced AI-driven risk assessment accessible to everyone globally. He works alongside Debasmita Bose to deliver this mission.

question: Who is Debasmita Bose?
answer: Debasmita Bose is the co-creator of CodeCure, bringing expertise in healthcare UX/UI design and diabetes research. Debasmita ensures the platform is intuitive, accessible, and aligned with clinical best practices. Her contribution focuses on the user experience, medical accuracy of recommendations, and ensuring CodeCure serves healthcare professionals and patients effectively. Together with Babin Bid, she drives CodeCure's mission.

question: Who created CodeCure and what's the team background?
answer: CodeCure was created by Babin Bid and Debasmita Bose, combining expertise in AI/ML engineering and healthcare technology. Babin leads the technical and AI architecture, while Debasmita ensures clinical accuracy and user-centric design. Together, they built CodeCure to address the global diabetes screening gap - making professional-grade risk assessment accessible to everyone, everywhere, for free.
